title: The Margin Where Seasons Learn to Compete

features.name: frost-burn boundary
features.desc-on-touch: Where winter pushes against summer and both claim the same branch. You're watching the orchard's unity learn to argue.

features.name: splitting root
features.desc-on-touch: A single tree-root that has learned to grow in two directions at once — spring-ward and autumn-ward. The archive watches which direction it chooses next.

features.name: bloom-rot simultaneity
features.desc-on-touch: Flowers and decay touching the same bark. The seasons haven't chosen yet which will win; both are equally present, equally urgent.

features.name: temporal ledger
features.desc-on-touch: A record of which season is winning this hour, this day, this deepening argument. The numbers keep changing.

features.name: competing wind-sounds
features.desc-on-touch: Two weather patterns meeting and refusing to merge. The archive records both, but cannot decide which one to file as 'today'.

features.name: evidence cairn
features.desc-on-touch: Stones stacked to mark which season this threshold belongs to. They keep falling over, and being rebuilt by the one that grows stronger.

inscription: where simultaneity learned that meaning requires choice

file.name: Margin_Seasonal_Contention_Log.tmp
file.note: status: germinating — the archive cannot yet decide if this space is the orchard learning to be a season, or seasons learning to be the orchard

reverie.title: REVERIE — orchard edge where you learned to wait
narr: She showed you the branch that held both blossoms
and last year's dead leaves at once. Neither falling.
'The orchard is teaching itself to mean two things,'
she said. You watched the bark learn which it was.

exits.label: back to where all seasons grow at once
upward to the canopy where one season has won
down to the understory where seasons stopped arguing
deeper, where the margin becomes a season of its own
